source_directive: lets have that same level of phase 5 intuiting for interactions as part of the phase 1 discovery. we need to make sure we analyze all designs so that when we do our route map and link the API to the front end, we have areas we know must be interactive and have guesses as to what they do. we need to present the user with a list of the mapping elements we have questions about (where its not sufficiently clear) and as a list, the user can sleect correct or not, then we ask about the ones we werent correct on.
layer_summary: All requirements are layer:infra — markdown/JSON skills, agents, commands, plus Python pytest structural tests. The architect-team plugin has no frontend and no backend; therefore no Playwright user-flow criteria and no dev-API integration criteria are required. Phase 5's `both`-layer integration discipline (real running backend) is N/A. Pipeline-meta work only.
playwright_n_a_authorization: The architect-team plugin self-modification has no UI to test. The codebase is markdown skills + Python hooks/tests + JSON manifests. Playwright user-flow criteria are N/A for every requirement. This authorization is consistent with prior plugin self-modifications (v0.9.18, v0.9.19, v0.9.20).
dev_api_n_a_authorization: The architect-team plugin has no backend API. Dev-API integration testing criteria are N/A for every requirement.

requirements.summary: Apply Phase-5-level interaction-intuition rigor at Phase −1 discovery time.
scenarios: skill `interaction-intuition` exists with correct frontmatter and required sections
agent `interaction-intuiter` exists with correct frontmatter and tools allowlist
INTERACTION_INTUITION_MAP.md schema documents every required frontmatter and per-element field
Confidence rubric defines high/medium/low/unknown with explicit criteria
Pipeline + intake-and-mapping skills name the intuiter in Phase −1B per-codebase mapping
ROUTE_MAP + DESIGN_MAP authoring skills name interaction-intuition as their downstream consumer
acceptance_criteria: pytest assertions for skill structure pass
pytest assertions for agent structure pass
schema-parametrize test exhaustively confirms field presence
wiring tests pass — pipeline skill, intake-and-mapping, frontend-route-mapping, design-fidelity-mapping, route-mapper all reference the new step in the expected location

requirements.summary: Present the user with a bulk-verify list of low-confidence intuitions; user picks the wrong ones; we drill down on those.
scenarios: Phase −1D section exists in pipeline skill with the canonical name
Trigger condition: gate fires iff at least one element is low/unknown/flagged-medium
Presentation format: numbered list with the documented per-item fields
Reply parser: three formats — `all correct`, integer list, `all incorrect`
Auto-confirmation: unflagged items get user_verdict=confirmed
Drill-down: one targeted question per flagged item, AskUserQuestion-batched 4-at-a-time, free-form fallback
Persist + re-mine when every flagged item resolved
acceptance_criteria: pytest wiring test asserts all of the above clauses are present in the pipeline skill body
skill body documents the three-format parser deterministically (regex-fallback-reprompt order)

requirements.summary: Confirmed intuitions become a binding input to Phase 0 + Phase 1.
scenarios: Phase 0 binding-input rule stated in pipeline skill
Override mechanism (`superseded_by`) defined
Phase 1 coverage-map enrichment rule for frontend/both-layer requirements stated
acceptance_criteria: pytest wiring test asserts the Phase 0 binding-input language is present
pytest wiring test asserts the Phase 1 coverage-map enrichment language is present

requirements.summary: The new bulk-verify gate does not contradict the v0.9.20 gates-are-opt-in rule.
scenarios: Pipeline skill's `## Default mode of operation` section distinguishes process gates from domain gates
Phase −1D, editability `ambiguous`, and interaction `ambiguous` escalations are explicitly named as domain gates
`commands/architect-team.md` --proposal-first flag bullet adds the one-line clarification
acceptance_criteria: pytest structural test parses `## Default mode of operation` and asserts the distinction is stated
pytest structural test asserts the command file's --proposal-first bullet contains the clarification

test_agents.py EXPECTED_AGENTS includes interaction-intuiter
Full suite at v0.9.21 baseline > 649; no regression
